dialogueChunks.intro: The outpost at {quest_focus} has sent word. The {enemy_type} are massing in numbers we haven't seen in a generation. The garrison there knows what's coming.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: There are eight soldiers up there. Eight. Against what scouts estimate is several hundred {enemy_type} pushing through {quest_origin}. The garrison commander sent a letter: not asking for reinforcements, just asking that someone look after their families. They've accepted what this means.
dialogueChunks.outro: Those soldiers are choosing to hold the line so {quest_stakes} have time to evacuate. If someone doesn't {quest_action}, their sacrifice buys hours instead of days. Every hour matters.

playerResponses.text: I won't let them die for nothing. I'll reinforce the outpost.
Eight soldiers can't hold anything. Focus on the evacuation.
I'm not walking into a last stand.

dialogueChunks.intro: They burned the library in {location} three weeks ago. Called it heresy. The scholar who maintained it vanished the same night.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: What the inquisitors don't know is that the scholar hid copies of the most important texts in {quest_origin} before the fires started. They knew {quest_threat} was coming and chose to protect the knowledge rather than themselves. {quest_focus} held centuries of learning: healing remedies, agricultural techniques, histories that certain people want forgotten.
dialogueChunks.outro: Someone needs to {quest_action} to a safe location before the inquisitors find the hiding spot. The scholar risked everything to preserve {quest_stakes}. It would be a tragedy if that sacrifice was wasted.

playerResponses.text: Knowledge shouldn't burn. Point me to the cellar.
If I'm caught with banned texts, it's my neck too.
I don't need the inquisitors on my trail.

dialogueChunks.intro: The healer at {quest_focus} locked herself in with the sick three days ago. She won't leave until they're cured or dead. The door is barred from the inside.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: She sent a list of what she needs through a gap in the wall: herbs, clean water, bandages. Simple things, but nobody will go near {quest_focus} because they're terrified of the sickness. She knows the risk. She chose the oath she swore over her own safety.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we don't {quest_action} soon, {quest_threat} is inevitable. The healer can save those people, but not without help from someone brave enough to approach the door. {quest_time_pressure}.

playerResponses.text: She's braver than anyone I know. I'll get her what she needs.
What's to stop the plague from spreading to me?
